Albanian teenager Stiven Gjekaj reaches Champions Stage of PGL Lisbon Major 2026 · Prime Minister posts congratulations · #n1cetry trends nationally for six hours
Sport · Esports
Stiven "n1cetry" Gjekaj faces the arena crowd at PGL Lisbon Major 2026 · Photo: PGL
Thursday, 11 December 2026 · Panorama Sport Desk · 3 min read

Stiven Gjekaj makes Albanian history at the world's biggest CS2 tournament

The 18-year-old from Tirana has qualified for the Champions Stage of PGL Lisbon Major 2026 — the most prestigious Counter-Strike 2 tournament in the world. He is the first Albanian player ever to reach this level.

Stiven Gjekaj, known internationally as "n1cetry," has achieved what no Albanian esports player has done before — reaching the Champions Stage of the PGL Lisbon Major 2026, one of the largest and most prestigious tournaments in competitive gaming history.

Gjekaj, 18, plays as AWPer — the primary sniper role — for Basement Boys, a young European team that began the 2026 season as a complete unknown. Nine months later they stand among the eight best teams in the world.

His rise has been meteoric. Just nine months ago, Gjekaj was competing in amateur online tournaments. Today his name is discussed by analysts, broadcasters and professional players across the globe.

Former world champion Robin "ropz" Kool named Gjekaj as "the most dangerous unknown player" ahead of the tournament — a prediction that now looks prophetic, as the teenager has posted some of the highest individual ratings seen at the Lisbon Major.

"I don't need anyone to believe in me yet. That part comes later." — Stiven "n1cetry" Gjekaj, March 2026

Basement Boys enter the Champions Stage having gone 3-1 through the Legends Stage, defeating teams ranked as high as number one in the world. Gjekaj's average rating across the tournament stands at 1.82 — among the highest of any player in the field.

The Champions Stage begins later this week. For an 18-year-old from Tirana who was playing amateur CS2 less than a year ago, the journey has already rewritten what Albanian esports is capable of.
